query: What are the fees associated with each milestone in the ERCOT generation interconnection process based on ERCOT fee schedule and resource interconnection handbook?
final_answer:
Now I have a comprehensive picture from the ERCOT Fee Schedule (effective October 1, 2024), the Resource Interconnection Handbook, and the Planning Guide. Let me compile the full answer.

Here is a comprehensive breakdown of the **fees associated with each milestone in the ERCOT Generation Interconnection Process**, based on the **ERCOT Fee Schedule (effective October 1, 2024)**, the **Resource Interconnection Handbook**, and the **ERCOT Planning Guide Section 5**.

---

## 🔌 ERCOT Generation Interconnection Process: Fee Schedule by Milestone

The interconnection process is divided into **three stages**, each with associated fees payable to ERCOT and/or the host Transmission/Distribution Service Provider (TDSP).

---

## 📋 STAGE 1: Interconnection Studies & Project Development

### Milestone 1.1 — Generation Interconnection or Change Request (GINR) Application
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| Submit GINR Application via RIOO-IS | Included with Screening Study fee |
| **Security Screening Study Fee** (paid with GINR application) | Based on capacity — refer to current ERCOT Fee Schedule posted on ERCOT.com (capacity-tiered) |

> 📌 *Note: The Screening Study fee is determined by the capacity (MW) of the proposed resource and must be paid at time of GINR submission. ERCOT has up to **90 calendar days** to complete the Screening Study. The specific dollar amount is updated periodically and referenced by the Resource Interconnection Handbook as found in the current ERCOT Fee Schedule.*

---

### Milestone 1.2 — Full Interconnection Study (FIS) Application
| Resource Type | Fee |
|---------------|-----|
| New Generator (≥ 10 MW) | **$3,000** |
| Modification to Existing Generator | **$2,700** |

> 📌 This fee is paid to ERCOT when the Interconnecting Entity (IE) requests a Full Interconnection Study (FIS), which is then assigned to the host TDSP. ERCOT has up to **180 days** to complete the FIS after the GINR. The FIS evaluates steady-state, protection, dynamic/transient stability, and sub-synchronous oscillation impacts.

---

### Milestone 1.3 — Execution of Standard Generation Interconnection Agreement (SGIA)
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| SGIA Negotiation & Execution (with host TDSP) | **TDSP-specific costs** (not set by ERCOT Fee Schedule) |
| ERCOT Review of SGIA Compliance | No additional ERCOT fee; up to **60 days** for review |

> 📌 The SGIA is executed between the IE and the host TDSP. Any construction costs for interconnection facilities (substation work, protection systems, etc.) are determined through the FIS and negotiated in the SGIA — these are project-specific and separate from ERCOT study fees.

---

### Milestone 1.4 — Quarterly Stability Assessment (QSA)
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| QSA (Conducted by ERCOT to assess the proposed resource's stability impact before synchronization) | No separate published fee — covered within interconnection process |

---

## 📋 STAGE 2: Resource Registration & Modeling

### Milestone 2.1 — Resource Entity (RE) Registration
| Entity Type | Fee |
|-------------|-----|
| Resource Entity Registration (new) | **$500** |
| QSE Registration (required to operate in ERCOT market) | **$500** |
| Counter-Party Background Check (per principal) | **$350/principal** |

---

### Milestone 2.2 — Generator Registration & Market Participant Setup

#### Generator Interconnection / Registration Fees (one-time, non-refundable):
| Generator Size/Type | Fee |
|---------------------|-----|
| Settlement Only Distribution Generator (SODG) | **$2,300** |
| Generator **under 10 MW** | **$8,000** |
| Generator **10 MW or greater** | **$14,000** |
| Change existing SODG to Distribution Generation Resource (DGR) | **$8,000** |
| New Load Resource Registration | **$500** |
| Modification to Load Resource (>20% MW increase or registration type change) | Additional **$500** |

#### Generator Modification Fees (cumulative MW within last 12 months):
| Modification Size | Fee |
|-------------------|-----|
| **Less than 10 MW** cumulative change | **$2,300** |
| **10 MW or greater** cumulative change | **$14,000** |

---

### Milestone 2.3 — Model Ready Date (MRD) / Resource Data Submission
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| Submit Resource Data in RIOO-IS (at FIS stage, Planning Model, and Full Registration) | No separate fee beyond registration fees above |

---

### Milestone 2.4 — EPS Meter / Telemetry Setup
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| Private WAN Installation/Reconfiguration (if applicable) | Actual cost, **capped at $7,000** |
| Monthly WAN Network Management Fee | Actual cost, **capped at $450/month** |

---

## 📋 STAGE 3: Energization, Synchronization & Commissioning

### Milestone 3.1 — Submit Commissioning Plan
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| Submit to ERCOT (via RIOO-IS) | No additional ERCOT fee |

---

### Milestone 3.2 — Part 1: Energization
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| ERCOT Review & Approval | No additional fee |

---

### Milestone 3.3 — Part 2: Initial Synchronization
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| Request for Initial Synchronization (up to 20 MVA and > 20 MVA) | No additional ERCOT fee |

---

### Milestone 3.4 — Part 3: Full Commercial Operation (COD)
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| Request to Commission Resource; weather preparedness declaration (if applicable) | No additional ERCOT fee |

---

## 🔁 Ongoing / Recurring Fees Post-Interconnection

| Fee Type | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| **Weatherization Inspection Fee** (Resource Entities, per TX Admin Code §25.55) | Proportional to MW capacity (calculated semiannually); invoiced in January and July; **late payments accrue 18% annual interest** |
| **Weatherization Inspection Fee** (TSPs — per inspected substation/switching station) | **$4,500 per substation** |
| **ERCOT Training** (Operator courses with NERC CEHs) | **$25 per NERC CEH** |
| **Information Requests** (requiring ERCOT staff time) | **$15/hour** for burdensome requests; actual cost with prior approval |
| **Voluminous Copy Fee** | **$0.15/page** (over 50 pages) |
| **Cybersecurity Monitor Fee** (non-ERCOT utilities in Texas Cybersecurity Monitor Program) | Varies annually (posted on ERCOT website) |

---

## ⚠️ Key Notes

1. **All ERCOT fees are non-refundable** unless otherwise specified by ERCOT Protocols.
2. **TDSP study costs** (System Impact Studies, facility studies) are separate and negotiated directly with the host TDSP — these are not part of the ERCOT Fee Schedule.
3. **Construction costs** for interconnection facilities are determined via the FIS and SGIA — project-specific.
4. The ERCOT interconnection process typically takes **18–30 months for large generation (≥10 MW)** and **8–12 months for small generation (<10 MW)**, not including construction/supply chain delays.
5. The ERCOT model is a **"connect and manage"** system — deliverability to load is NOT guaranteed through the interconnection process; it's managed via congestion in real-time operations.
6. The Security Screening Study fee (not explicitly itemized in the retrieved fee schedule pages) is capacity-dependent and should be confirmed directly against the **current ERCOT Fee Schedule** posted at [ercot.com](https://www.ercot.com/services/rq/integration).

---

*Sources: ERCOT Fee Schedule (effective October 1, 2024), Resource Interconnection Handbook (v1.94+), ERCOT Planning Guide Section 5, and ERCOT Nodal Protocols Section 16.*

   - Provides interval data for each channel associated with an ESI ID.

2. **Treatment of ERCOT Polled Settlement Resource ID Data**:
   - ERCOT is identified as the MRE (Meter Reading Entity).
   - Models and populates data to ensure netting and aggregation conform to ERCOT Protocols.
   - Makes RID interval and SCADA data available to relevant entities.

   - Provides interval data to TSPs/DSPs by noon on the tenth business day after reading the EPS meter.

3. **Treatment of ERCOT-Polled Settlement Energy Storage Resource Load Data**:

### Key Sections:

#### 11.1 Overview
- ERCOT collects interval data from EPS metered entities using the Meter Data Acquisition System (MDAS).
- The data is validated, edited, estimated, adjusted, netted, loss corrected, split, and aggregated for settlement inputs.

#### 11.1.2 ERCOT Polled Settlement Meter Data Collection
- ERCOT remotely interrogates EPS metered entities to gather necessary data for settlement.
- The MDAS verifies the meter’s Interval Data Recorder (IDR) protocol and device identifier.
- If remote polling fails, ERCOT collaborates with Transmission Service Providers (TSP) and Distribution Service Providers (DSP) to resolve issues within the timeframe specified in Section 10.

#### 11.1.3 ERCOT Polled Settlement Meter Time Synchronization
- ERCOT updates the clock of any EPS meter outside the defined threshold.
- TSP and DSP assist in correcting timekeeping inconsistencies within the specified timeframe.

#### 11.1.4 ERCOT Polled Settlement Meter Data Validation, Editing, and Estimation
- After synchronization and data retrieval, ERCOT validates the data.
- Validation includes flagging intervals with missing data and exception reporting if zero values exceed tolerance limits.",

The corrected data will be used in load aggregation, and NOIEs can access this data via the Market Information System.

3. **Treatment of Non-Opt-In Entity or External Load Serving Entity Radially Connected Entities (11.1.9):**
   - For NOIE or ELSE metering points, where the TSP or DSP supplies data to ERCOT, each delivery point will be treated as an individual ESI ID if the data is not bi-directional.

4. **Treatment of ERCOT Polled Settlement Load Data (11.1.10):**
   - ERCOT will:
     - Use data for settlement calculations and reports.
     - Provide daily kWh consumption information to TSP, DSP, and LSE, following Texas SET 867_03 standards.
     - Allow for retail switching via standard processes.
     - Be identified as the Meter Reading Entity.

1. **Variance Process (11.3.2):**

2. **Alternative Dispute Resolution (11.3.3):**
   - Entities may seek correction of ESI ID service history/usage information and resettlement through the Alternative Dispute Resolution Procedure outlined in Section 20.

        "text": "The document outlines the calculation of the Initial Estimated Liability (IEL) for Counter-Parties in the ERCOT market, based on their Qualified Scheduling Entities (QSEs). 

1. **For Counter-Parties with QSEs representing only Resources:**
   - The IEL is calculated using the formula: 
     \[
     \text{IEL} = \text{DEG} \times \max(0.2, \text{RTEFG}) \times \text{RTAEP} \times (\text{M1} + \text{M2})
     \]
   - Variables:
     - **IEL**: Initial Estimated Liability in dollars.
     - **DEG**: Daily Estimated Generation in MWh.
     - **RTEFG**: Real-Time Energy Factor for Generation.
     - **RTAEP**: Real-Time Average Energy Price in $/MWh.

2. **For Counter-Parties with QSEs representing both Load and Resources:**
   - The IEL is calculated using the formula:
     \[
     \text{IEL} = \text{DEL} \times \max(0.1, \text{RTEFL}) \times \text{RTAEP} \times (\text{M1} + \text{M2}) + \text{DEG} \times \max(0.1, \text{RTEFG}) \times \text{RTAEP} \times (\text{M1} + \text{M2})
     \]
   - Variables:
     - **DEL**: Daily Estimated Load in MWh.
     - **RTEFL**: Real-Time Energy Factor for Load.

3. **For Counter-Parties with QSEs representing neither Load nor generation:**
   - The IEL is equal to the Initial Market Credit Exposure (IMCE) as defined in another section.

An updated definition includes Energy Storage Resources (ESR) and Settlement Only Energy Storage Systems (SOESS).

5. **Interval Data Recorder (IDR) Meter Data Threshold:** Specifies the percentage of IDR Meter data required by a Meter Reading Entity (MRE) before ERCOT can perform a True-Up Settlement, as detailed in a specific section.

The page is part of a public document detailing protocols for energy management and settlement processes.",

tool_input.query: ERCOT fee schedule 2024 security screening study fee interconnection large small generator site:ercot.com

steps.content:
Now I have a comprehensive picture from the ERCOT Fee Schedule (effective October 1, 2024), the Resource Interconnection Handbook, and the Planning Guide. Let me compile the full answer.

Here is a comprehensive breakdown of the **fees associated with each milestone in the ERCOT Generation Interconnection Process**, based on the **ERCOT Fee Schedule (effective October 1, 2024)**, the **Resource Interconnection Handbook**, and the **ERCOT Planning Guide Section 5**.

---

## 🔌 ERCOT Generation Interconnection Process: Fee Schedule by Milestone

The interconnection process is divided into **three stages**, each with associated fees payable to ERCOT and/or the host Transmission/Distribution Service Provider (TDSP).

---

## 📋 STAGE 1: Interconnection Studies & Project Development

### Milestone 1.1 — Generation Interconnection or Change Request (GINR) Application
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| Submit GINR Application via RIOO-IS | Included with Screening Study fee |
| **Security Screening Study Fee** (paid with GINR application) | Based on capacity — refer to current ERCOT Fee Schedule posted on ERCOT.com (capacity-tiered) |

> 📌 *Note: The Screening Study fee is determined by the capacity (MW) of the proposed resource and must be paid at time of GINR submission. ERCOT has up to **90 calendar days** to complete the Screening Study. The specific dollar amount is updated periodically and referenced by the Resource Interconnection Handbook as found in the current ERCOT Fee Schedule.*

---

### Milestone 1.2 — Full Interconnection Study (FIS) Application
| Resource Type | Fee |
|---------------|-----|
| New Generator (≥ 10 MW) | **$3,000** |
| Modification to Existing Generator | **$2,700** |

> 📌 This fee is paid to ERCOT when the Interconnecting Entity (IE) requests a Full Interconnection Study (FIS), which is then assigned to the host TDSP. ERCOT has up to **180 days** to complete the FIS after the GINR. The FIS evaluates steady-state, protection, dynamic/transient stability, and sub-synchronous oscillation impacts.

---

### Milestone 1.3 — Execution of Standard Generation Interconnection Agreement (SGIA)
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| SGIA Negotiation & Execution (with host TDSP) | **TDSP-specific costs** (not set by ERCOT Fee Schedule) |
| ERCOT Review of SGIA Compliance | No additional ERCOT fee; up to **60 days** for review |

> 📌 The SGIA is executed between the IE and the host TDSP. Any construction costs for interconnection facilities (substation work, protection systems, etc.) are determined through the FIS and negotiated in the SGIA — these are project-specific and separate from ERCOT study fees.

---

### Milestone 1.4 — Quarterly Stability Assessment (QSA)
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| QSA (Conducted by ERCOT to assess the proposed resource's stability impact before synchronization) | No separate published fee — covered within interconnection process |

---

## 📋 STAGE 2: Resource Registration & Modeling

### Milestone 2.1 — Resource Entity (RE) Registration
| Entity Type | Fee |
|-------------|-----|
| Resource Entity Registration (new) | **$500** |
| QSE Registration (required to operate in ERCOT market) | **$500** |
| Counter-Party Background Check (per principal) | **$350/principal** |

---

### Milestone 2.2 — Generator Registration & Market Participant Setup

#### Generator Interconnection / Registration Fees (one-time, non-refundable):
| Generator Size/Type | Fee |
|---------------------|-----|
| Settlement Only Distribution Generator (SODG) | **$2,300** |
| Generator **under 10 MW** | **$8,000** |
| Generator **10 MW or greater** | **$14,000** |
| Change existing SODG to Distribution Generation Resource (DGR) | **$8,000** |
| New Load Resource Registration | **$500** |
| Modification to Load Resource (>20% MW increase or registration type change) | Additional **$500** |

#### Generator Modification Fees (cumulative MW within last 12 months):
| Modification Size | Fee |
|-------------------|-----|
| **Less than 10 MW** cumulative change | **$2,300** |
| **10 MW or greater** cumulative change | **$14,000** |

---

### Milestone 2.3 — Model Ready Date (MRD) / Resource Data Submission
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| Submit Resource Data in RIOO-IS (at FIS stage, Planning Model, and Full Registration) | No separate fee beyond registration fees above |

---

### Milestone 2.4 — EPS Meter / Telemetry Setup
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| Private WAN Installation/Reconfiguration (if applicable) | Actual cost, **capped at $7,000** |
| Monthly WAN Network Management Fee | Actual cost, **capped at $450/month** |

---

## 📋 STAGE 3: Energization, Synchronization & Commissioning

### Milestone 3.1 — Submit Commissioning Plan
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| Submit to ERCOT (via RIOO-IS) | No additional ERCOT fee |

---

### Milestone 3.2 — Part 1: Energization
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| ERCOT Review & Approval | No additional fee |

---

### Milestone 3.3 — Part 2: Initial Synchronization
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| Request for Initial Synchronization (up to 20 MVA and > 20 MVA) | No additional ERCOT fee |

---

### Milestone 3.4 — Part 3: Full Commercial Operation (COD)
| Action | Fee |
|--------|-----|
| Request to Commission Resource; weather preparedness declaration (if applicable) | No additional ERCOT fee |

---

## 🔁 Ongoing / Recurring Fees Post-Interconnection

| Fee Type | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| **Weatherization Inspection Fee** (Resource Entities, per TX Admin Code §25.55) | Proportional to MW capacity (calculated semiannually); invoiced in January and July; **late payments accrue 18% annual interest** |
| **Weatherization Inspection Fee** (TSPs — per inspected substation/switching station) | **$4,500 per substation** |
| **ERCOT Training** (Operator courses with NERC CEHs) | **$25 per NERC CEH** |
| **Information Requests** (requiring ERCOT staff time) | **$15/hour** for burdensome requests; actual cost with prior approval |
| **Voluminous Copy Fee** | **$0.15/page** (over 50 pages) |
| **Cybersecurity Monitor Fee** (non-ERCOT utilities in Texas Cybersecurity Monitor Program) | Varies annually (posted on ERCOT website) |

---

## ⚠️ Key Notes

1. **All ERCOT fees are non-refundable** unless otherwise specified by ERCOT Protocols.
2. **TDSP study costs** (System Impact Studies, facility studies) are separate and negotiated directly with the host TDSP — these are not part of the ERCOT Fee Schedule.
3. **Construction costs** for interconnection facilities are determined via the FIS and SGIA — project-specific.
4. The ERCOT interconnection process typically takes **18–30 months for large generation (≥10 MW)** and **8–12 months for small generation (<10 MW)**, not including construction/supply chain delays.
5. The ERCOT model is a **"connect and manage"** system — deliverability to load is NOT guaranteed through the interconnection process; it's managed via congestion in real-time operations.
6. The Security Screening Study fee (not explicitly itemized in the retrieved fee schedule pages) is capacity-dependent and should be confirmed directly against the **current ERCOT Fee Schedule** posted at [ercot.com](https://www.ercot.com/services/rq/integration).

---

*Sources: ERCOT Fee Schedule (effective October 1, 2024), Resource Interconnection Handbook (v1.94+), ERCOT Planning Guide Section 5, and ERCOT Nodal Protocols Section 16.*
